questions.q: What is the central theme of the poem 'The Lake Isle of Innisfree'?
questions.explanation: The poem expresses the speaker's desire to escape to a peaceful and serene environment, which is a longing for solitude.

questions.q: Who is the narrator in the poem 'The New Colossus'?
questions.explanation: The poem is written in the first person, and the speaker is a poet who is describing the Statue of Liberty.

questions.q: What is the significance of the title 'The Lady of Shalott'?
opts: (A) The lady is a symbol of beauty
(B) The lady is a symbol of isolation
(C) The lady is a symbol of love
(D) The lady is a symbol of death
questions.explanation: The title 'The Lady of Shalott' emphasizes the lady's isolation and her inability to connect with the outside world.

questions.q: What is the tone of the poem 'Ozymandias'?
questions.explanation: The poem's tone is melancholic, as it describes the decline and fall of a great civilization.

questions.q: Who is the protagonist in the poem 'Do Not Go Gentle Into That Good Night'?
questions.explanation: The speaker addresses his father throughout the poem, urging him to resist his death.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the poem 'The Hollow Men'?
opts: (B) To express a sense of despair
questions.explanation: The poem expresses a sense of despair and disillusionment with modern life.

questions.q: What is the significance of the word 'emptiness' in the poem 'The Hollow Men'?
opts: (A) It represents a sense of happiness
(B) It represents a sense of peace
(C) It represents a sense of despair
(D) It represents a sense of excitement
questions.explanation: The word 'emptiness' emphasizes the speaker's feeling of desolation and hopelessness.

questions.q: Who is the speaker in the poem 'The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock'?
questions.explanation: The poem is written in the first person, and the speaker is the poet himself, T.S. Eliot.

questions.q: What is the theme of the poem 'The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock'?
questions.explanation: The poem explores the speaker's anxiety and indecision in social situations, which is a common experience in modern life.

questions.q: What is the symbolism of the sea in the poem 'Sea-Fever'?
questions.explanation: The sea is a symbol of freedom and the speaker's desire to escape the monotony of life.

questions.q: Who is the speaker in the poem 'The Second Coming'?
questions.explanation: The poem is written in the first person, and the speaker is the poet himself, W.B. Yeats.

questions.q: What is the theme of the poem 'The Second Coming'?
opts: (A) The rise of a new world order
questions.explanation: The poem explores the idea that a great civilization is in decline and that a new, more chaotic era is emerging.

questions.q: What is the significance of the phrase 'the centre cannot hold' in the poem 'The Second Coming'?
opts: (A) It represents the power of nature
(B) It represents the rise of a new world order
(C) It represents the decline of civilization
(D) It represents the joy of love
questions.explanation: The phrase 'the centre cannot hold' emphasizes the idea that the old social and moral order is breaking down.

questions.q: Who is the speaker in the poem 'The Waste Land'?
questions.explanation: The poem is written in the first person, and the speaker is the poet himself, T.S. Eliot.

questions.q: What is the theme of the poem 'The Waste Land'?
questions.explanation: The poem explores the idea that modern life is empty, fragmented, and devoid of spiritual meaning.

questions.q: What is the significance of the phrase 'these fragments I have shored against my ruins' in the poem 'The Waste Land'?
opts: (A) It represents the power of nature
(B) It represents the rise of a new world order
(C) It represents the decline of civilization
(D) It represents the speaker's attempt to make sense of his fragmented world
questions.explanation: The phrase emphasizes the speaker's attempt to make sense of his fragmented world and to find meaning in the ruins of modern life.

questions.q: Who is the speaker in the poem 'The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock and Other Poems'?
questions.explanation: The poem is written in the first person, and the speaker is the poet himself, T.S. Eliot.

questions.q: What is the theme of the poem 'The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock and Other Poems'?
questions.explanation: The poem explores the idea that modern life is characterized by anxiety, indecision, and a sense of disconnection.

questions.q: What is the significance of the image of the 'yellow fog' in the poem 'The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock'?
opts: (A) It represents the beauty of nature
(B) It represents the power of love
(C) It represents the anxiety of modern life
(D) It represents the speaker's sense of disconnection
questions.explanation: The image of the 'yellow fog' emphasizes the speaker's sense of disconnection and isolation in modern life.

questions.q: Who is the speaker in the poem 'The Hollow Men'?
questions.explanation: The poem is written in the first person, and the speaker is the poet himself, T.S. Eliot.

questions.q: What is the theme of the poem 'The Hollow Men'?
questions.explanation: The poem explores the idea that modern life is empty, fragmented, and devoid of spiritual meaning.
